steps.title: Identify what the blank must signal
steps.content: The passage introduces 180 sunspots in November 2001, then uses the blank to introduce a contrasting example: 2 sunspots in December 2008. These are opposite extremes — high versus low — that together prove sunspot numbers "can vary greatly." The blank must signal a contrast or comparison between two data points.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In other words,': signals restatement, implying the second example says the same thing as the first in different words. Two opposite numbers are not a restatement — wrong logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Similarly,': signals equivalence or parallel similarity. Placing a near-zero count next to a 180-count as "similar" contradicts the passage's point about variation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Therefore,': signals a causal result. The low 2008 count is not caused by the high 2001 count — wrong logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'By comparison,': signals a direct contrast between two data points, which is exactly the structure the sentence requires to show the range of variation. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
